description: Refactor from code-driven architecture to Agent-driven architecture with BaseTool, AgentLoop, and structured output

tasks.title: Agent infrastructure layer
tasks.description: Create AgentConfig, BaseTool, ToolResult, and StructuredOutput utility classes as the foundation for multi-agent architecture.
acceptanceCriteria: AgentConfig dataclass exists with fields: name, model, base_url, api_key, temperature, max_tokens, max_loop_turns, system_prompt, enable_thinking
AgentConfig(enable_thinking=False).extra_body returns {"chat_template_kwargs": {"enable_thinking": False}}
AgentConfig(enable_thinking=True).extra_body returns {}
ToolResult Pydantic model exists with fields: success (bool), data (Any), error (str|None)
ToolResult has to_llm_message(tool_call_id) method that returns valid tool role message dict
BaseTool is an abstract class with name, description, parameters class attributes
BaseTool has execute() method that wraps _execute() in try/except and always returns ToolResult
BaseTool has to_openai_tool() method that returns valid OpenAI tools JSON schema
BaseTool subclass can be created by implementing _execute()
StructuredOutput class has static parse() method that accepts model_class, messages, config
parse() uses client.beta.chat.completions.parse() or response_format fallback
parse() handles errors gracefully and returns fallback value
tasks.notes: New files: services/agent_config.py, services/tool_base.py, services/llm_utils.py. No existing code modified.
verification: uv run python3 -c 'from services.agent_config import AgentConfig; c = AgentConfig(name="test", enable_thinking=False); assert c.extra_body == {"chat_template_kwargs": {"enable_thinking": False}}'
uv run python3 -c 'from services.agent_config import AgentConfig; c = AgentConfig(name="test", enable_thinking=True); assert c.extra_body == {}'
uv run python3 -c 'from services.tool_base import ToolResult; t = ToolResult(success=True, data="ok"); assert t.to_llm_message("call_1")["role"] == "tool"'
uv run python3 -c 'from services.llm_utils import StructuredOutput'
BaseTool subclass execute() never throws, test with intentionally failing _execute()

tasks.title: Agent loop and tool definitions
tasks.description: Create AgentLoop class with tool-calling decision loop using native OpenAI tools parameter. Define at least 2 concrete tools (normal_chat, search_resumes) as BaseTool subclasses.
acceptanceCriteria: AgentLoop class exists with run(user_input, history, tools, config) method
AgentLoop uses native OpenAI tools parameter for tool-calling decisions
AgentLoop returns LLM text response when no tool_calls are made
AgentLoop executes tool functions and appends results to messages when tool_calls are present
AgentLoop has MAX_TURNS configurable via AgentConfig.max_loop_turns
AgentLoop exceeds MAX_TURNS returns timeout/fallback message
SearchResumesTool exists as BaseTool subclass
SearchResumesTool._execute() calls existing resume_searcher + keyword_extractor
SearchResumesTool returns ToolResult(success=True, data=[...])
NormalChatTool exists as instruction for LLM to respond without searching
All tools have valid to_openai_tool() JSON schema output
tasks.notes: New files: services/agent_loop.py, services/tools/__init__.py, services/tools/normal_chat.py, services/tools/search_resumes.py
verification: uv run python3 -c 'from services.tools.search_resumes import SearchResumesTool; t=SearchResumesTool(); assert t.to_openai_tool()["function"]["name"]=="search_resumes"'
uv run python3 -c 'from services.agent_loop import AgentLoop; assert hasattr(AgentLoop, "run")'
AgentLoop E2E: search request triggers search_resumes tool, chat request uses normal_chat, weather question uses normal_chat

tasks.title: Existing code migration to agent architecture
tasks.description: Migrate keyword_extractor, resume_handler, text_handler, and config.py to use the new agent infrastructure.
acceptanceCriteria: keyword_extractor.extract_keywords() output is KeywordExtraction Pydantic object
KeywordExtraction has fields: keywords (list[str]), should_search (bool)
keyword_extractor uses StructuredOutput.parse() internally
resume_handler LLM analysis output is split into ResumeDisplay (for user) and ResumeMeta (for database)
ResumeDisplay has: summary, education, experience, skills (all str)
ResumeMeta has: structured fields for database indexing (name, sex, phone, school, major, degree, skills, etc.)
text_handler integrates AgentLoop for text messages
AgentLoop tools in text_handler include search_resumes and normal_chat
config.py has chat_agent, keyword_agent, analysis_agent AgentConfig fields
All AgentConfig fields can be overridden via environment variables
Docker build succeeds
Existing functionality (PDF upload, chat, session management) preserved
tasks.notes: Modified config.py, services/keyword_extractor.py, services/handlers/resume_handler.py, services/handlers/text_handler.py. E2E tested with real LLM.
verification: uv run python3 -c 'from services.keyword_extractor import KeywordExtraction; print("OK")'
uv run python3 -c 'from services.handlers.resume_handler import ResumeDisplay, ResumeMeta; print("OK")'
uv run python3 -c 'from config import Config; c=Config(); assert c.chat_agent.name=="chat"'
Integration: extract_keywords(search_query) returns keywords, extract_keywords(chat) returns empty
